title: PLO and PA Terror Payments Accountability Act of 2025
short_title: PLO and PA Terror Payments Accountability Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill requires the President to punish people and groups that help the Palestine Liberation Organization and Palestinian Authority pay money to terrorists and their families. The payments go to people who committed terrorist attacks against Americans or Israelis, died doing terrorist attacks, or are family members of these terrorists. The punishments include freezing their money in the US, not letting them visit America, and stopping banks from helping them. The President must also punish anyone who helps these people or groups in important ways. These punishments will only stop if the US government says the Palestinian groups have completely ended their payment system to terrorists.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the PLO and PA Terror Payments Accountability Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings Statement of Policy
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. First, the Palestine Liberation Organization and the Palestinian Authority give hundreds of millions of dollars every year to terrorists and their families. This payment system encourages and rewards terrorism. Second, these organizations have laws and rules that require payments to terrorists, including members of groups that the United States has officially labeled as terrorist organizations. These groups include Hamas and Islamic Jihad, which receive support from Iran. Third, in 2018 Congress passed the Taylor Force Act to try to stop these payments. This law limits United States aid to the Palestinian Authority unless they stop paying terrorists and change their laws. Fourth, even after the Taylor Force Act became law, the Palestine Liberation Organization and Palestinian Authority have continued making payments to terrorists. Fifth, on October 7, 2023, Hamas, Islamic Jihad, and other terrorist groups from Gaza attacked Israel during a Jewish holiday. This was the deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust. Sixth, on October 7, 2023, Hamas fired thousands of rockets at Israeli civilians and sent thousands of terrorists into Israeli communities. These terrorists killed, raped, tortured, burned alive, and kidnapped Israelis and Americans, including children, babies, and elderly people. Some were Holocaust survivors. Children were murdered in front of their parents and parents were murdered in front of their children. Seventh, on October 7, 2023, Hamas murdered more than one thousand two hundred people, mostly civilians, and kidnapped more than two hundred forty people, taking them to Gaza as hostages. The United States policy is to hold the Palestine Liberation Organization and the Palestinian Authority responsible by imposing sanctions for giving money to terrorists and their families as part of a system that encourages terrorism.

sections.title: Section 3  Definitions
sections.text: This section explains what certain terms mean in this law. An act of terrorism means international terrorism as defined in federal law, including terrorist activity. Appropriate congressional committees means specific committees in both the House of Representatives and the Senate that deal with foreign affairs, banking, and legal matters. A foreign person means any person or organization that is not a United States person. Knowingly means that a person actually knew something or should have known it. System of compensation means the payment system described in the Taylor Force Act where the Palestinian Authority and Palestine Liberation Organization give money to terrorists and their families. A United States person means a United States citizen, a permanent resident, a company organized under United States law, or a person in the United States.

sections.title: Section 4  Imposition of Sanctions on Certain Foreign Persons Supporting Terrorism
sections.text: Within ninety days of this law taking effect, and continuing after that, the President must impose sanctions on certain people and organizations. These sanctions apply to any foreign person who has worked as a representative, minister, official, or employee of the Palestine Liberation Organization or Palestinian Authority and helped with their terrorist payment system. They also apply to anyone who has given payments to terrorists and their families as part of this system. Sanctions also apply to organizations that have operated or controlled the terrorist payment system, including specific named groups and any related organizations. Finally, sanctions apply to any foreign person who has knowingly provided significant support to people already described or engaged in important transactions with them. The sanctions include two main parts. First, the President must block and freeze all property and money belonging to these people if it is in the United States or controlled by Americans. Second, these people cannot get visas to enter the United States, cannot be admitted to the United States, and any current visas they have will be cancelled immediately. If someone violates these rules, they face the same penalties as people who break other similar economic laws. When congressional committee leaders ask whether someone meets the criteria for sanctions, the President has thirty days to decide and report back. The President can use emergency economic powers to carry out this section and must issue regulations within sixty days.

sections.title: Section 5  Imposition of Sanctions with Respect to Financial Institutions That Facilitate Transactions Supporting Terrorism
sections.text: Within ninety days of this law taking effect, and continuing after that, the President must impose sanctions on foreign banks and financial institutions that help with terrorist payments. These sanctions apply to foreign financial institutions that process or help with payments to terrorists or their families, or that knowingly handle significant financial transactions with people already under sanctions. The sanction means the President must prohibit these foreign banks from opening accounts in the United States or must put strict conditions on any accounts they already have. The law defines correspondent accounts and payable-through accounts using existing banking definitions, and defines foreign financial institutions using the definition from previous Iran sanctions laws.

sections.title: Section 6  Termination
sections.text: This law will stop having any effect only if the Secretary of State writes a certification to the appropriate congressional committees. The certification must state that the Palestine Liberation Organization and Palestinian Authority payment system that gives money to terrorists and their families has completely stopped and is no longer happening.
